title: Information Security & Data Privacy
description: Data classification, password standards, MFA, GDPR privacy principles, and security incident reporting.

articles.title: Data Classification Tiers & Handling Standards
articles.content:
Data Classification Tiers:
1. Public: Approved for external release (press releases, public rates).
2. Internal: For internal Bank use only (memos, org charts). No external distribution.
3. Confidential: Sensitive business data (client portfolios, deal terms). Minimum control: Encryption at rest and in transit, access logging.
4. Restricted: Highest sensitivity (client identity documents, MNPI, credentials). Minimum control: Encryption, need-to-know access, DLP monitoring.
Access Management: Granted strictly on need-to-know basis via IAM. Access reviewed quarterly (UAR). Revoked within 24 hours of exit (immediately for involuntary termination).

articles.title: Password Standards, MFA & Remote Access Controls
articles.content:
Password & Authentication Controls:
• Minimum Password Length: 14 characters.
• Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): Mandatory for all remote access and privileged (admin) accounts.
• Password History: Last 12 passwords cannot be reused.
• Account Lockout: Locked after 5 failed attempts within 15 minutes.
• Session Timeout: 15 minutes of inactivity for systems handling Confidential or Restricted data.
Acceptable Use & Remote Work: Use Bank devices for business. Personal email or unsanctioned cloud storage (personal Dropbox/Gmail) to store/send Restricted data is strictly prohibited. Remote workers must connect via approved VPN / Zero Trust and lock screens when unattended.

articles.title: Data Privacy Rights (GDPR) & SOC Incident Reporting
articles.content:
Data Privacy Principles (GDPR):
• Processing must follow lawfulness, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, and integrity.
• Data Subject Rights: Any request (access, rectification, erasure) must be forwarded to the Data Privacy Office within 24 hours.
Security Incident Reporting (Report to Security Operations Centre / SOC 24/7 Hotline):
• Suspected Phishing / Malware: Report immediately to SOC.
• Lost or Stolen Device: Report within 1 hour to SOC + IT Service Desk for remote wipe.
• Confirmed Personal Data Breach: Report immediately to Data Privacy Office + SOC + MLRO.
• Unauthorised Restricted Access: Report immediately to SOC + Data Owner.
Note: Do NOT use unapproved AI tools or browser extensions for Confidential/Restricted data.
